findings.evidence: Lines 112-115 first derive `hden` from `hz hw`, then the rest of the proof is only `rw [pseudoHyperbolicDist, norm_eq_zero, div_eq_zero_iff]; simp only [hden, or_false]`; grep also shows the already more general denominator lemma at lines 53-56.
findings.fix: Add/prove the general lemma `pseudoHyperbolicDist_eq_zero_iff_of_den_ne_zero {z w : ℂ} (hden : 1 - (starRingEnd ℂ) w * z ≠ 0) : pseudoHyperbolicDist z w = 0 ↔ z = w`, then derive the norm/unit-disc versions from it.
findings.issue: `pseudoHyperbolicDist_eq_zero_iff_of_norm_lt_one` assumes `‖z‖ < 1` and `‖w‖ < 1`, but the proof only needs `1 - (starRingEnd ℂ) w * z ≠ 0`; the current signature is stronger than the standard denominator-nonzero form for a total division expression.

summary: The API is mostly at the intended scalar/unit-disc level, but one public zero-characterization bakes in unit-disc assumptions where the natural reusable hypothesis is just denominator nonvanishing.
